summary: 2026-07-08 19:16:06 PDT - Reviewed `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, `git diff main...HEAD`, and `reviews/simple-default-role-gate.md`. The branch mostly matches the role-gated Simple-first spec, but I found one availability edge where Simple mode can still compute from a non-manual live count while showing the empty attendee prompt.

findings.title: Unavailable Simple mode can still accrue from cached live count
findings.claim: `App` now hides the participants-unavailable blocker in Simple mode, but it still passes `participants.length` as `liveCount` into `selectActiveTotals`. When `simpleUserCount` is blank, `selectActiveTotals` uses that live count, while the new `simpleCountDisplay` hides the count whenever `participantsAvailable` is false. If the adapter reports the list unavailable after a prior non-empty participant snapshot, the attendee field is empty but the meter accrues cost for cached attendees, violating AC2b/AC4's requirement that unavailable Simple mode calculate from the manual count and read $0 until filled.
findings.suggestion: Use an availability-aware live count for Simple mode, e.g. pass `participantsAvailable ? participants.length : 0` or teach the totals helper about `participantsAvailable`, and make the attendee-count commit compare against the live count only when the list is available. Add a regression test for `participantsAvailable: false`, blank `simpleUserCount`, and nonzero `liveCount`.
